title: H3K18 lactylation drives ferroptosis resistance and EGFR-TKI tolerance in lung adenocarcinoma
abstract: Lactate accumulation in the tumor microenvironment promotes therapy resistance. Here we show that lactate-derived H3K18 lactylation upregulates SLC7A11 to suppress ferroptosis in EGFR-TKI-tolerant lung adenocarcinoma cells. Pharmacologic inhibition of lactate production restored TKI sensitivity in vivo. Our findings identify histone lactylation as a metabolic-epigenetic axis and a therapeutic vulnerability in drug-tolerant cancer.

title: Single-cell RNA sequencing reveals a prognostic immune signature in hepatocellular carcinoma
abstract: We performed scRNA-seq on 24 hepatocellular carcinoma samples and identified eight major cell populations. A T-cell-exhaustion gene signature stratified patients into high- and low-risk groups predictive of overall survival. The signature was validated in TCGA and correlated with response to immune checkpoint blockade, providing a prognostic biomarker for clinical decision-making.

title: DNA methylation of GLP-1R promoter is associated with type 2 diabetes risk in a case-control cohort
abstract: Promoter hypermethylation can silence gene expression. In a cohort of 412 patients and 400 controls, we found that methylation at three CpG sites in the GLP-1R promoter was significantly associated with type 2 diabetes status (p<0.01). Methylation correlated with reduced peripheral GLP-1R expression. These associations suggest GLP-1R methylation as a candidate epigenetic biomarker for diabetes risk.

title: Integration of ATAC-seq and RNA-seq reveals epigenetic regulation of heavy-metal tolerance in rice
abstract: Rice (Oryza sativa) faces cadmium stress in contaminated soils. We profiled chromatin accessibility (ATAC-seq) and transcriptomes across cadmium exposure and identified accessible regions enriched near stress-response transcription factor genes. A regulatory module centered on OsbHLH was associated with tolerance, informing breeding strategies for safer crops in contaminated environments.

title: Histone demethylase MoRph1 regulates fungal development and pathogenicity in Magnaporthe oryzae
abstract: The rice blast fungus Magnaporthe oryzae causes devastating crop losses. We characterized MoRph1, a JmjC histone demethylase, and show that its deletion impairs conidiation, appressorium formation, and virulence. RNA-seq revealed dysregulation of pathogenicity genes. MoRph1 represents a potential antifungal target for protecting cereal crops.

title: A deep learning framework for predicting in vivo G-quadruplex formation from sequence
abstract: G-quadruplexes (G4s) are non-canonical DNA structures. We present iDualG4, a dual-channel convolutional-recurrent neural network that predicts G4 formation from primary sequence with improved accuracy over existing tools across multiple cell lines. The model generalizes to unseen genomes and is provided as a web server for the community.
